mnemonics.title: BCNF Definition Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: BCNF: 'Big Cats Need Furry' - B=Big (Boyce), C=Codd (C), N=Need, F=Functional dependencies, Y=Yield superkeys. Remember: Left side must be SuperKey.

mnemonics.title: Normalization Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: NFs Come For Properly Executing Queries - 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, BCNF, 4NF, 5NF/PJNF (Properly joins need function (F) for queries)

mnemonics.title: BCNF vs 3NF Difference
mnemonics.content: 3NF: Left OR Right (Right can be prime). BCNF: Left must be KEY (only superkey). 3NF = OR, BCNF = KEY.

acronyms.usage: Higher level of normalization introduced by Boyce and Codd in 1974 to eliminate anomalies not resolved by 3NF
Describes relationship between attributes where one attribute determines another
A chosen candidate key used to uniquely identify tuples in a relation
Minimal superkey - minimal set of attributes that uniquely identifies all tuples
Set of attributes that uniquely identifies all tuples in a relation

visualTips.visualization: Think of a table being split into two smaller tables: one with the violating FD (X,Y) and one with remaining attributes. Draw the original table as a rectangle, then show the 'cut' along the FD line.
Draw concentric circles: outer = superkey, inner = candidate key. Candidate keys are minimal - remove any attribute and it's no longer a key.
Draw a Venn diagram: large circle = all FDs, smaller circle = 3NF violations, smallest circle = BCNF violations. BCNF violations are a subset of 3NF violations.

analogies.analogy: Like separating mixed nuts into two jars: one with the violating dependency (like almonds and cashews that shouldn't be together) and one with the rest. The goal is clean separation while keeping everything usable.
Like a country's citizen ID - knowing the ID tells you the person's name, address, and other details. The ID uniquely determines all other information.
Like taking a torn photo and placing pieces back together - if done correctly (lossless), you get the exact original photo with no gaps or extras.
Like keeping instruction manuals - if you can follow rules in each room separately without having to go back to the original manual, dependencies are preserved.
